name: useTransition for Non-Urgent Updates
description: Wrap state updates that cause expensive re-renders in startTransition to keep the UI responsive. React can interrupt and restart the update if higher-priority work arrives.

name: useDeferredValue for Expensive Derived State
description: Defer an expensive derived computation while keeping the input responsive. The deferred value lags behind the current value during high-priority renders.

name: use() Hook for Promises and Context
description: React 19's use() hook reads a Promise or Context inside render — enabling server-to-client data passing without useEffect. Suspense catches the pending state.

name: React Server Actions
description: Server Actions are async functions that run on the server, called directly from Client Components or HTML forms. They replace API routes for mutations.

name: useOptimistic for Optimistic UI
description: Apply an optimistic state update immediately while the async operation is in flight. React automatically reverts on error.

name: Compound Component Pattern
description: Build flexible, composable UI by splitting a component into a parent (holds state) and named child components (consume via context). Consumer controls the layout.

name: React.memo, useMemo, useCallback — When and When Not
description: Memoization is a tradeoff. Overusing it adds memory and maintenance cost. Apply only when profiler shows a real problem.

name: Error Boundaries
description: Use error boundaries to catch render errors in a subtree and show a fallback UI instead of crashing the whole app. Required around every async boundary.

name: Custom Hook Patterns
description: Extract stateful logic into custom hooks for reuse across components. Hooks should be pure logic — no JSX, no side effects beyond React's own.

name: TanStack Query for Server State
description: Use TanStack Query (React Query) to manage all async server state. Eliminates useEffect/useState data-fetching boilerplate and adds caching, deduplication, and background refetch.
